title: Rosa longicruris
intro: Rosa longicruris, also known as the Long Stalked Rose, is a flowering plant species in the Rosaceae family. The Long Stalked Rose is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may help to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Rosa longicruris include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Long Stalked Rose is traditionally used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to support digestive health and soothe skin irritations.
taxonomy: Rosa longicruris is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It belongs to the genus Rosa, which includes many other species of roses. The specific epithet 'longicruris' is derived from Latin, where 'longus' means long and 'cruris' means leg, likely referring to the plant's characteristics. As a member of the Rosaceae family, Rosa longicruris is closely related to other plants such as apples and pears. Further classification may be possible at the subspecies or varietal level, but this information is not readily available.

constituents: Rosa longicruris, being a species of wild rose, contains various bioactive compounds that contribute to its medicinal properties. These active constituents include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and carotenoids, which are known for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. The plant's extracts are also rich in essential oils, such as geraniol and linalool, which possess antimicrobial and antifungal properties.
